answer:
**Fichte argues that freedom of thought (Denkfreiheit) is an inalienable human right inherent to personhood and derived directly from the moral law and divine endowment, not from any grant by princes or the state.**[^1] Princes have hitherto suppressed it and merely pretend to "permit" or "grant" it—when in reality, they cannot prevent thinking itself (as it is internal and free), though they forbid its communication.[^2] Since society cannot alienate this right in the social contract (as doing so would degrade humans to animals), it cannot transfer any power over it to princes; thus, princes have no legitimate right to suppress, grant, or revoke it.[^3] Instead, as the work's title declares, it must be **reclaimed** from princes who have usurped and suppressed it.[^4]

Fichte addresses the princes' claim directly:

> \"But do you even want that?\" they cry out to us; \"Have we not loudly and solemnly enough given you permission to think freely?\" And we will concede this; we will forget the anxious attempts made to deprive us of the best means; we will forget with what diligence they seek to color the old darkness in every new light; we will not quibble over words—**yes, you permit us to think, since you cannot prevent it**; but you forbid us to communicate our thoughts; thus, you do not claim our inalienable right to think freely, you merely claim the right to prevent us from communicating what we have freely thought.[^5]

He concludes unequivocally:

> **Prince, you have no right to suppress our freedom of thought: and what you have no right to do, you must never do**, even if the worlds around you perish, and you and your people should be buried under their ruins.[^6]

[^1]: Johann Gottlieb Fichte, *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens*, ch. 2, [p-5ec8d2](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02/translation/#p-5ec8d2), [p-900390](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02/translation/#p-900390), [p-e62dae](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02/translation/#p-e62dae).

[^2]: Johann Gottlieb Fichte, *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens*, ch. 2, [p-b0279a](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02/translation/#p-b0279a).

[^3]: Johann Gottlieb Fichte, *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens*, ch. 2, [p-be3fae](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02/translation/#p-be3fae), [p-c72eae](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02/translation/#p-c72eae), [p-7062a4](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02/translation/#p-7062a4).

[^4]: Johann Gottlieb Fichte, *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens*, ch. 1, [p-effd89](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/01/translation/#p-effd89).

[^5]: Johann Gottlieb Fichte, *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens*, ch. 2, [p-b0279a](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02/translation/#p-b0279a).

[^6]: Johann Gottlieb Fichte, *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens*, ch. 2, [p-44e1de](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02/translation/#p-44e1de).
